heading: Imposition of sanctions with respect to theft of trade secrets of United States persons
label: 1709 Imposition of sanctions with respect to theft of trade secrets of United States persons

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Protecting American Intellectual Property Act of 2022, and not as part of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act which comprises this chapter.

Delegation of Functions and Authorities Under the Protecting American Intellectual Property Act of 2022Memorandum of President of the United States, , 89 F.R. 64345, provided:July 19, 2024Memorandum for the Heads of Executive Departments and AgenciesBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code, I hereby order as follows:section 301 of title 31. (a) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State, with input from the Director of National Intelligence, the Attorney General, the Secretary of Commerce, and the heads of other relevant executive departments and agencies (agencies), as appropriate, the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 2(a) of the Protecting American Intellectual Property Act of 2022 (; ) (the “Act”). To support the Secretary of State’s preparation of the report required under section 2(a) of the Act, the Director of National Intelligence, the Attorney General, the Secretary of Commerce, and, as appropriate, the heads of other relevant agencies, shall provide pertinent information to the Secretary of State at least 90 days before the deadline for submission of the annual report to the appropriate congressional committees.SectionPublic Law 117–33650 U.S.C. 1709(b) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State, in consultation with the heads of other relevant agencies, as appropriate, the authority to select the types of sanctions to be imposed under section 2(b)(1) of the Act. Once applicable sanctions are selected pursuant to section 2(b)(1) of the Act, I hereby delegate to the heads of other relevant agencies the authority to implement such sanctions, commensurate with their respective areas of responsibility.(c) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of the Treasury the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 2(b)(2)(A) and section 2(d) of the Act.(d) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 2(c) of the Act.2. The delegations in this memorandum shall apply to any provisions of any future public laws that are the same or substantially the same as the provisions referenced in this memorandum. Any reference in this memorandum to the Act shall be deemed to be a reference to such Act as amended from time to time.Sec.3. The Secretary of State is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.Sec.J.R. Biden, Jr.

Delegation of Functions and Authorities Under the Protecting American Intellectual Property Act of 2022Memorandum of President of the United States, , 89 F.R. 64345, provided:July 19, 2024Memorandum for the Heads of Executive Departments and AgenciesBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code, I hereby order as follows:section 301 of title 31. (a) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State, with input from the Director of National Intelligence, the Attorney General, the Secretary of Commerce, and the heads of other relevant executive departments and agencies (agencies), as appropriate, the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 2(a) of the Protecting American Intellectual Property Act of 2022 (; ) (the “Act”). To support the Secretary of State’s preparation of the report required under section 2(a) of the Act, the Director of National Intelligence, the Attorney General, the Secretary of Commerce, and, as appropriate, the heads of other relevant agencies, shall provide pertinent information to the Secretary of State at least 90 days before the deadline for submission of the annual report to the appropriate congressional committees.SectionPublic Law 117–33650 U.S.C. 1709(b) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State, in consultation with the heads of other relevant agencies, as appropriate, the authority to select the types of sanctions to be imposed under section 2(b)(1) of the Act. Once applicable sanctions are selected pursuant to section 2(b)(1) of the Act, I hereby delegate to the heads of other relevant agencies the authority to implement such sanctions, commensurate with their respective areas of responsibility.(c) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of the Treasury the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 2(b)(2)(A) and section 2(d) of the Act.(d) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 2(c) of the Act.2. The delegations in this memorandum shall apply to any provisions of any future public laws that are the same or substantially the same as the provisions referenced in this memorandum. Any reference in this memorandum to the Act shall be deemed to be a reference to such Act as amended from time to time.Sec.3. The Secretary of State is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.Sec.J.R. Biden, Jr.

children.label: i
children.text: has knowingly engaged in, or benefitted from, significant theft of trade secrets of United States persons, if the theft of such trade secrets occurred on or after , and is reasonably likely to result in, or has materially contributed to, a significant threat to the national security, foreign policy, or economic health or financial stability of the United States;January 5, 2023

children.label: ii
children.text: has provided significant financial, material, or technological support for, or goods or services in support of or to benefit significantly from, such theft;

children.label: iii
children.text: is an entity that is owned or controlled by, or that has acted or purported to act for or on behalf of, directly or indirectly, any foreign person identified under clause (i) or (ii); or

children.label: iv
children.text: is a chief executive officer or member of the board of directors of any foreign entity identified under clause (i) or (ii);

children.label: B
children.text: describing the nature, objective, and outcome of the theft of trade secrets each foreign person described in subparagraph (A)(i) engaged in or benefitted from; and

children.label: C
children.text: assessing whether any chief executive officer or member of the board of directors described in clause (iv) of subparagraph (A) engaged in, or benefitted from, activity described in clause (i) or (ii) of that subparagraph.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of the first report required by paragraph (1), the period beginning on Janaury 5, 2023, and ending on the date on which the report is required to be submitted; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of each subsequent report required by paragraph (1), the one-year period preceding the date on which the report is required to be submitted.

children.heading: Form of report
children.label: 3 Form of report
children.text: Each report required by paragraph (1) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may include a classified annex.

children.heading: Blocking of property
children.label: A Blocking of property
children.text: The President may, pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.), block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property of the entity if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Inclusion on entity list
children.label: B Inclusion on entity list
children.text: The President may include the entity on the entity list maintained by the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce and set forth in Supplement No. 4 to part 744 of the Export Administration Regulations, for activities contrary to the national security or foreign policy interests of the United States.

children.heading: Export-Import bank assistance for exports to sanctioned persons
children.label: C Export-Import bank assistance for exports to sanctioned persons
children.text: The President may direct the Export-Import Bank of the United States not to give approval to the issuance of any guarantee, insurance, extension of credit, or participation in the extension of credit in connection with the export of any goods or services to the entity.

children.heading: Loans from United States financial institutions
children.label: D Loans from United States financial institutions
children.text: The President may prohibit any United States financial institution from making loans or providing credits to the entity totaling more than $10,000,000 in any 12-month period unless the person is engaged in activities to relieve human suffering and the loans or credits are provided for such activities.

children.heading: Loans from international financial institutions
children.label: E Loans from international financial institutions
children.text: The President may direct the United States executive director to each international financial institution to use the voice and vote of the United States to oppose any loan from the international financial institution that would benefit the entity.

children.heading: Prohibition on designation as primary dealer
children.label: i Prohibition on designation as primary dealer
children.text: Neither the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System nor the Federal Reserve Bank of New York may designate, or permit the continuation of any prior designation of, the financial institution as a primary dealer in United States Government debt instruments.

children.heading: Prohibition on service as a repository of government funds
children.label: ii Prohibition on service as a repository of government funds
children.text: The financial institution may not serve as agent of the United States Government or serve as repository for United States Government funds.

children.heading: Procurement sanction
children.label: G Procurement sanction
children.text: The United States Government may not procure, or enter into any contract for the procurement of, any goods or services from the entity.

children.heading: Foreign exchange
children.label: H Foreign exchange
children.text: The President may, pursuant to such regulations as the President may prescribe, prohibit any transactions in foreign exchange that are subject to the jurisdiction of the United States and in which the entity has any interest.

children.heading: Banking transactions
children.label: I Banking transactions
children.text: The President may, pursuant to such regulations as the President may prescribe, prohibit any transfers of credit or payments between financial institutions or by, through, or to any financial institution, to the extent that such transfers or payments are subject to the jurisdiction of the United States and involve any interest of the entity.

children.heading: Ban on investment in equity or debt of sanctioned person
children.label: J Ban on investment in equity or debt of sanctioned person
children.text: The President may, pursuant to such regulations or guidelines as the President may prescribe, prohibit any United States person from investing in or purchasing significant amounts of equity or debt instruments of the entity.

children.heading: Exclusion of corporate officers
children.label: K Exclusion of corporate officers
children.text: The President may direct the Secretary of State to deny a visa to, and the Secretary of Homeland Security to exclude from the United States, any alien that the President determines is a corporate officer or principal of, or a shareholder with a controlling interest in, the entity.

children.heading: Sanctions on principal executive officers
children.label: L Sanctions on principal executive officers
children.text: The President may impose on the principal executive officer or officers of the entity, or on individuals performing similar functions and with similar authorities as such officer or officers, any of the sanctions under this paragraph.

children.heading: Blocking of property
children.label: A Blocking of property
children.text: The President shall, pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.), block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property of the alien if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
children.label: B Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
I
children.text: inadmissible to the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: ineligible to receive a visa or other documentation to enter the United States; and

children.label: III
children.text: otherwise ineligible to be admitted or paroled into the United States or to receive any other benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et seq.).8 U.S.C. 1101

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: An alien described in subparagraph (A) of subsection (a)(1) is subject to revocation of any visa or other entry documentation regardless of when the visa or other entry documentation is or was issued.

children.label: aa
children.text: take effect pursuant to section 221(i) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (); and8 U.S.C. 1201(i)

children.label: bb
children.text: cancel any other valid visa or entry documentation that is in the alien’s possession.
determines that such a waiver is in the national interests of the United States; and
not more than 15 days after issuing the waiver, submits to the appropriate congressional committees a notification of the waiver and the reasons for the waiver.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1704) to carry out this section.50 U.S.C. 1702

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 2 Penalties
children.text: A person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of this section or any regulation, license, or order issued to carry out this section shall be subject to the penalties set forth in subsections (b) and (c) of section 206 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act () to the same extent as a person that commits an unlawful act described in subsection (a) of that section.50 U.S.C. 1705

children.heading: Intelligence activities
children.label: 1 Intelligence activities
children.text: This section shall not apply with respect to activities subject to the reporting requirements under title V of the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.) or any authorized intelligence activities of the United States.50 U.S.C. 3091

children.heading: Law enforcement activities
children.label: 2 Law enforcement activities
children.text: Sanctions under this section shall not apply with respect to any authorized law enforcement activities of the United States.

children.heading: Exception to comply with international agreements
children.label: 3 Exception to comply with international agreements
children.text: Sanctions under this section shall not apply with respect to the admission of an alien to the United States if such admission is necessary to comply with the obligations of the United States under the Agreement regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , between the United Nations and the United States, or the Convention on Consular Relations, done at Vienna , and entered into force , or other international obligations.June 26, 1947November 21, 1947April 24, 1963March 19, 1967

children.heading: Exception relating to importation of goods
children.label: 4 Exception relating to importation of goods

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The authority or a requirement to impose sanctions under this section shall not include the authority or a requirement to impose sanctions on the importation of goods.

children.heading: Good defined
children.label: B Good defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “good” means any article, natural or manmade substance, material, supply, or manufactured product, including inspection and test equipment, and excluding technical data.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: f Sunset
children.text: This section shall terminate on the date that is 7 years after .January 5, 2023

children.heading: Admission; admitted; alien; lawfully admitted for permanent residence
children.label: 1 Admission; admitted; alien; lawfully admitted for permanent residence
children.text: The terms “admission”, “admitted”, “alien”, and “lawfully admitted for permanent residence” have the meanings given those terms in section 101 of the Immigration and Nationality Act ().8 U.S.C. 1101

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Financial Services and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Entity
children.label: 3 Entity
children.text: The term “entity” means a partnership, association, trust, joint venture, corporation, group, subgroup, or other organization.

children.heading: Export Administration Regulations
children.label: 4 Export Administration Regulations
children.text: The term “Export Administration Regulations” has the meaning given that term in .section 4801 of this title

children.heading: Foreign entity
children.label: 5 Foreign entity
children.text: The term “foreign entity” means an entity that is not a United States person.

children.heading: Foreign person
children.label: 6 Foreign person
children.text: The term “foreign person” means any person that is not a United States person.

children.heading: Knowingly
children.label: 7 Knowingly
children.text: The term “knowingly”, with respect to conduct, a circumstance, or a result, means that a person has actual knowledge, or should have known, of the conduct, the circumstance, or the result.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 8 Person
children.text: The term “person” means an individual or entity.

children.heading: Trade secret
children.label: 9 Trade secret
children.text: The term “trade secret” has the meaning given that term in .section 1839 of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: a United States citizen or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence to the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: an entity organized under the laws of the United States or of any jurisdiction within the United States, including a foreign branch of such an entity; or

children.label: C
children.text: any person in the United States.
